prompt: A feature uploads invoices and must (a) read fields from varied, novel layouts and (b) re-check arithmetic totals that are already printed on the invoice. Applying the 'fit is a spectrum' idea, which statements are correct? Select ALL that apply.
choices: The varied-layout field reading is the irreducibly fuzzy core that genuinely fits AI
Re-checking known arithmetic totals is exact work best done in deterministic code
Narrowing the AI surface to only the fuzzy step makes the system cheaper, more testable, and more swappable
Both steps should be sent to the model so the technology stays uniform

prompt: A team's hosted-API extraction feature has grown from 2,000 to 900,000 calls per month, accuracy needs have risen, and they now hold a year of labeled data. Why might re-sourcing toward a fine-tuned model be justified? Select ALL that apply.
choices: At real volume the per-call API price has become the largest cost line, a TCO concern
Accumulated labeled data now makes fine-tuning viable where it was not before
Sourcing is reversible and should be revisited as volume, accuracy needs, and data change
A larger per-call price is always preferable because it signals higher quality

prompt: An architect wants the build-vs-buy decision to stay reversible and lock-in low. Which design choices support that goal? Select ALL that apply.
choices: Put the provider behind an abstraction layer so it can be swapped without rewriting the product
Keep prompts and evaluation suites portable across providers
Maintain an exit plan the team can actually execute
Adopt the most proprietary vendor format available to maximize switching costs
